feedback:
I was drinking coffee as I was reading it, haha!

Also the word "Everyday" is incorrect. (That is an adjective to describe something else like it's an "everyday" thing. For example "it's an everyday habit" (describing habit).  What you should say is "every day" (it's an adverb of time). So, "How often do you drink coffee?" Answer: "Every day." (I teach English as a second language and this is something I have to explain but even native speakers get this wrong).

Pref_process: As much as I value organically grown items, I refuse to participate in the USDA 'certified organic' labeling.  It's a scam.  It shouldn't cost a farmer thousands of dollars just to have a label like that.  Plus, coffee grown conventionally is more than likely just as good as 'certified' organic coffee, if not better, since the only difference is the 'certified organic' paid a lot of money just to have that label and charge twice as much for the product.  There's no guarantee it's actually even organic.
